name: vigilante-get-vulnerable-host-data

outputs.description: Container for vulnerable host objects. This is basically a list of dictionaries (hosts). VigilanteATI.Hosts.ip Alphanumeric version of the IPv4 address belonging to this vulnerable host.
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts

outputs.description: Hostname of the host. Obtained by doing a lookup using the given IPv4 address. (e.g "sample.hostname.com") VigilanteATI.Hosts.type Vulnerability type/name. (e.g heartbleed)
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts.hostname

outputs.description: Port numbers that this might affect. (e.g 443) VigilanteATI.Hosts.protocols Protocols that this might affect. (e.g "ssh")
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts.port

outputs.description: URL field. Only applicable with certain vulnerability types such as web shell. VigilanteATI.Hosts.geoip JSon of all the data we have about the IP and its location.
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts.url

outputs.description: Contient Code (e.g AS) VigilanteATI.Hosts.geoip.city_name City Name (e.g Tokyo)
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts.geoip.continent_code

outputs.description: Country Code (e.g JP) VigilanteATI.Hosts.geoip.country_name Country Name (e.g Japan)
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts.geoip.country_code2

outputs.description: Country Code (e.g JP) VigilanteATI.Hosts.geoip.region_name Region Name(e.g Tokyo)
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts.geoip.country_code3

outputs.description: date Timestamp of when we found this vulnerable host and imported it into our database. (e.g 2015-10-10 12:01:01) VigilanteATI.GetVulnerableHostsToken
outputs.name: VigilanteATI.Hosts.timestamp

outputs.description: The re_token parameter value required to retrieve the next page of results. The token is valid for 5 minutes. The value is null if count is equal to either 0 or total.
outputs.name: unknown
